svg.title: Patient tooth history workflow with file references
svg.desc: Detailed workflow diagram for the patient tooth history view, with improved spacing and file references for every major step.
svg.text: Improved spacing: panels no longer overlap, long text is wrapped manually, and each box keeps a file reference so you can jump straight into the code.
After profile load, tooth clicks are local UI updates
The request starts in the patients workspace.
The tooth chart is not on screen yet.
1. User clicks View or Edit in the patients table
This picks a patient row and starts the profile workflow.
Reads `state.companySlug`, the provided `patientId`, or the current profile id.
Then delegates the actual HTTP call to `apiRequest(...)`.
The browser builds one authenticated GET request.
That single request asks for the full profile payload.
The URL combines the active tenant slug and the selected patient id.
Reads `response.text()`, parses JSON when needed, and throws on non-OK responses.
The request survives tenant resolution, JWT auth, route checks,
and controller guards before business logic runs.
Stores tenant context for the request.
The endpoint requires a valid JWT.
Allowed roles: Owner, Admin, Manager, Employee.
Tenant endpoints are role-protected before BLL executes.
This is the main backend workflow that builds
patient info, tooth state, and tooth history payload.
Reads the patient with `AsNoTracking().SingleOrDefaultAsync(...)` and throws if missing.
for all permanent teeth when the chart is incomplete.
This is the only optional write in the read workflow.
Loads current tooth status, notes, and timestamps for this patient.
Loads only treatments that have `ToothNumber`, includes `TreatmentType`,
Groups treatment history by tooth number and creates one `PatientToothResult`
The service returns patient info plus `teeth[]`, and each tooth already contains `history[]`.
These are the tables and EF Core behaviors that shape
the patient profile payload behind the scenes.
that the current user has a valid tenant role.
Provides the patient identity row used as the profile root object.
Stores one row per tooth with latest condition, notes, and audit timestamps.
Missing rows can be created by `EnsureFullToothChartAsync`.
The human-readable procedure name comes from `TreatmentType`.
The service reads newest treatment first.
`AppDbContext` limits tenant entities to the active `CompanyId`.
Soft-deleted rows are excluded from the normal profile queries.
So the profile cannot leak cross-tenant or deleted data.
applies audit fields, soft delete rules, and audit logs.
So the fallback chart seeding still follows platform rules.
The controller converts the BLL result into the JSON shape
The response includes patient basics plus `teeth[]`, and each tooth includes `history[]`.
After the JSON response comes back, the rest of the experience
is local UI state and DOM rendering.
Stores `state.patientProfile`, computes counts, fills summary facts,
Chooses preferred tooth -> first with history -> first issue -> first tooth.
Builds the chart UI, hover text, and tooth history panel from
Clicking another tooth re-renders locally. No second API call is needed.
401 in `apiRequest`, 403 for tenant or role issues, 404 for missing patient, and ProblemDetails from middleware on server-side exceptions.
